content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 17,
1992
Honorable Francie Sullivan
Shasta County Supervisor
Attn:
Pat O’Brien
Administrative Assistant
1815
Yuba Street,
Suite
1
Redding,
CA
96001
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—92—126
Dear Ms.
Sullivan:
You have requested advice concerning your responsibilities as
a Shasta County Supervisor pursuant to the provisions of the
Political Reform Act
(the “Act”)
1 governing the receipt of gifts.
QUESTION
May you receive
a gift of travel
from the Associated
California Loggers to attend the National Association of Counties
annual convention in Washington D.C.?
CONCLUSION
Reimbursement for actual travel expenses and reasonable
subsistence in connection with the conference may be accepted and
would be exempt
from the $1,000 limit on gifts and honoraria
applicable to local elected officials.
However,
absent
some express exception in the Act, all public
officials must disclose the
name and address of each source of
gifts and honoraria of
$50 or more in value,
the amount and the
date on which the gift was received,
and a general description of
the business activity of the donor.
Moreover,
a public official
may not make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use
his or her official position to influence
a governmental decision
which will have
a material financial effect,
distinguishable from
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California
Code of Regulations Section
18000,
et g.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 •
(916)322-5660


Our File No.
A-92—126
Page
2
its effect on the public generally,
on any donor of,
or any
intermediary or agent for a donor of, gifts or honoraria
aggregating $250 or more in value provided to,
received by,
or
promised to the public official within
12 months prior to the time
when the decision is made.
DISCUSSION
You are
a local elected official.
In our telephone
conversation of February
19,
1992,
we discussed whether you were
prohibited from receiving a payment from the Associated California
Loggers for travel to the National Association of Counties annual
convention.
As
we discussed, the Act does not prohibit the
receipt of gifts by public officials.
However,
Section 89501
provides:
No local elected officeholder shall accept any
honoraria for any speech given, article published,
or attendance at any public or private conference,
convention, meeting,
social event,
meal,
or like
gathering,
or any gifts,
from any single source,
which is in excess of one thousand dollars
($1,000),
in any calendar year,
except
reimbursement for actual travel expenses and
reasonable subsistence in connection therewith.
Based on this section local elected officers are now subject
to a $1,000 aggregate limit for gifts and honoraria received from
any single source.
However,
the statute specifically excludes
from the $1,000 limit reimbursement for actual travel expenses and
reasonable subsistence in connection with a speech given, article
published,
or attendance at any public or private conference,
convention,
meeting,
social event,
meal,
or like gathering.
Thus
the reimbursement for your travel expenses by the Associated
California Loggers would not be considered for purposes of the
$1,000 gift limit in Section 89501.
Please note,
however,
as we discussed,
absent some express
exception in the Act, all public officials must disclose the name
and address of each source of gifts of
$50 or more in value,
the
amount and the date
on which the gift was received,
and a general
description of the business activity of the donor.
(Section
87207.)
Moreover,
yOu are prohibited from making, participating in
making or in any way attempting to use your official position to
influence a governmental decision which will have a reasonably
foreseeable material financial effect, distinguishable from its
effect on the public generally,
on any donor of,
or any
intermediary or agent for a donor of,
a gift or gifts aggregating
$250 or more in value provided to,
received by,
or promised to you
within
12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.
(Sections 87100,


Our File No.
A—92—126
Page
3
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322_590l.2
Sincerely,
SH:JWW:aa
Scott Hallabrin
ing General Counsel
John
W.
Wallace
Counsel,
Legal Division
2
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively,
copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 1O per page.
